name: Loom
tagline: Instant video messaging for async workplace communication
description: Record and share video messages instantly with screen, cam, or both
pros: Instant recording with shareable link reducing meetings by 30%
AI auto-generated titles, chapters, and transcripts
Viewer engagement analytics with completion and rewatch data
Reaction emoji and comment threading for async feedback
Password protection, domain whitelisting, and expiration controls
cons: Free tier limited to 25 videos and 5-minute recordings
No native desktop editor beyond basic trimming
Enterprise SSO only on Business plan at $12.50/creator/month

content.title: Executive Summary
content.body: Record and share video messages instantly with screen, cam, or both Loom earns a 4.5/5 rating from 38,000 user reviews.

content.title: TL;DR
content.body: Loom is a leading video communication platform rated 4.5/5 across 38,000 reviews. Pricing starts at Free–$12.50/creator/mo. Best for Remote Teams and Sales Teams.

content.title: Rating Overview
content.body: Loom scores 4.5/5 overall with ease of use (4.7/5), features (4.3/5), performance (4.5/5), support (4.2/5), value (4.5/5).

content.title: Quick Verdict
content.body: Loom earns a 4.5/5 rating recommended for Remote Teams and Sales Teams. Delivers strong feature depth with intuitive interface.

content.title: Company Background
content.body: Loom was founded in 2016 in San Francisco, USA, serving 25M+ users customers with 400+ employees.

content.title: Product Overview
content.body: Loom is a instant video messaging for async workplace communication. Provides 18 features across Core, AI, Analytics, Security, Desktop, Mobile categories. Mobile on iOS and Android. API available.

content.title: Key Features
content.body: Essential video communication features:
items: Screen Recording: Cam, screen-only, or both.
Custom Branding: Logo and color personalization.
Call to Action: End-screen link buttons.

content.title: Feature Deep Dive
content.body: Screen Recording. Cam, screen-only, or both. AI Transcripts. Auto-generated searchable text. AI Chapters. Smart section breakpoints.

content.title: AI Features
content.body: Loom AI includes AI video chapters, Auto-generated transcripts, Magic fill.

content.title: API & Automation
content.body: Loom provides a comprehensive API for custom integration and workflow automation.

content.title: Security
content.body: Loom holds SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA certifications with TLS 1.2+ and AES-256 encryption.

content.title: Compliance
content.body: Loom complies with GDPR, CCPA, SOC 2 regulations.

content.title: Integrations
content.body: Integrates with Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Jira, Confluence, Asana, Trello, Salesforce, and 2+ more.

content.title: Pricing
content.body: Loom uses freemium with per-creator premium. Free tier available. Plans from Free–$12.50/creator/mo.

content.title: Pros
content.body: Loom advantages:
items: Instant recording with shareable link reducing meetings by 30%
AI auto-generated titles, chapters, and transcripts
Viewer engagement analytics with completion and rewatch data
Reaction emoji and comment threading for async feedback

content.title: Cons
content.body: Considerations:
items: Free tier limited to 25 videos and 5-minute recordings
No native desktop editor beyond basic trimming
Enterprise SSO only on Business plan at $12.50/creator/month

content.title: Performance
content.body: Strong performance with fast load times.

content.title: Ease of Use
content.body: Loom has a low learning curve with intuitive interface.

content.title: User Experience
content.body: Streamlined modern interface.

content.title: Customer Support
content.body: Support rated 4.2/5 via email and knowledge base.

content.title: Industry Fit
content.body: Best for Video and Communication with Remote Teams and Sales Teams teams.

content.title: Team Size Fit
content.body: Loom accommodates teams of all sizes with generous free tier. Best ROI for 5-50 person teams.

content.title: Real Use Cases
content.body: A remote teams team uses Loom to streamline video communication workflows and improve team productivity.

content.title: Competitor Analysis
content.body: Loom competes with other platforms. Primary differentiators: feature depth and ease of use.

content.title: Alternatives
content.body: Consider alternatives based on specific team requirements.

content.title: Migration Guide
content.body: Migration rated low complexity. Most teams transition within 1-2 weeks. Parallel testing recommended.

content.title: Buying Advice
content.body: Evaluate: feature alignment, total cost at Free–$12.50/creator/mo, migration effort (1-2 weeks), and real-data trial.

content.title: Final Verdict
content.body: Loom earns 4.5/5. Strong choice for Remote Teams and Sales Teams. Recommended for teams prioritizing video communication.

faqs.question: What is Loom best used for?

author: PilotStack Team
learningCurve: Moderate — expect 1-3 days to become productive
whoShouldUse: Loom is best suited for enterprises and power users who need advanced video communication capabilities. Teams with dedicated IT support will get the most from its extensive feature set.
whoShouldNotUse: Loom is less ideal for small teams on tight budgets, as its advanced features come at a premium price point. Freelancers may find more cost-effective alternatives.
